problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Microbiome modulation via targeted bacteriophage therapy
hypothesis: Microbiome modulation via targeted bacteriophage therapy may resolve Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Human organoid model with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
